name: Tashkent
summary: Tashkent is one of the safest capitals in Central Asia with low crime rates and friendly locals. The US State Department maintains a Level 1 travel advisory for Uzbekistan, indicating standard precautions are sufficient. While natural hazard risks exist due to seismic activity, modern infrastructure and emergency services are well-developed in the capital.
quickVerdict: Tashkent stands out as a remarkably safe destination for travelers, with consistently low crime rates and a strong security presence throughout the city. Solo female travelers frequently report feeling safer here than in many European capitals, and the city's well-developed public transport system, including the stunning Soviet-era metro, provides secure mobility options. While visitors should remain vigilant for tourist-targeted scams and respect local Islamic customs, the warm hospitality of locals and the government's focus on tourism safety make Tashkent an excellent introduction to Central Asia.

neighborhoods.name: City Center/Amir Temur Square
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Tashkent featuring government buildings, hotels, and major attractions. Heavy security presence makes this one of the safest areas, though tourist-targeting scams are more common near major landmarks.

neighborhoods.name: Chilanzar District
neighborhoods.description: Well-developed residential area with good municipal services and infrastructure. Popular with locals and safe for tourists, though it's more densely populated with fewer English speakers.

neighborhoods.name: Yunusabad District
neighborhoods.description: Modern district with shopping centers and business areas. Generally very safe with good lighting and regular security patrols, making it popular for evening walks.

neighborhoods.name: Old City (Eski Shahar)
neighborhoods.description: Historic area with traditional architecture and local markets. Safe during daylight hours but some narrow streets have limited lighting after dark. Rich cultural experience with friendly locals.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Factory districts on city outskirts with fewer amenities and security services. Not unsafe but less patrolled and poorly lit at night. Better to avoid unless specifically needed.

neighborhoods.name: Sergeli District
neighborhoods.description: Developing residential area that has seen some reported incidents. Generally safe during day but exercise standard precautions, especially in areas further from main roads and transport hubs.

scams.name: Taxi Fare Scams
scams.description: Unlicensed taxi drivers quote inflated prices or claim the fare is per person rather than per trip. Some drivers make unauthorized stops to pick up accomplices for robbery.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official Yandex Go app, agree on price before departure, ensure price is for the entire trip not per person. Avoid unmarked taxis and always use licensed services.

scams.name: Restaurant Bill Manipulation
scams.description: Restaurants near tourist sites add items not ordered, claim menu prices changed, or add unauthorized service charges up to 50%. Some establishments bring complimentary items then charge for them.
scams.howToAvoid: Check menu and prices before ordering, verify the bill carefully before paying, ask about service charges upfront. Count your change and keep receipts.

scams.name: Found Money Scam
scams.description: Stranger approaches claiming to have found cash on the street and offers to split it with you, but only after you contribute your own money to the scheme.
scams.howToAvoid: Ignore approaches from strangers offering deals involving money. If someone claims to find money, politely decline involvement and walk away immediately.

scams.name: Fake Camera Damage
scams.description: Local asks tourist to take their photo, then deliberately drops camera when it's returned and demands payment for damage.
scams.howToAvoid: Be cautious when handling strangers' electronics. If asked to take photos, suggest they use your phone instead or politely decline if situation feels suspicious.

soloFemale.overview: Tashkent is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, with many women reporting feeling safer than in European capitals. Harassment is rare, and locals are generally respectful and helpful. The strong police presence and cultural values of hospitality create a secure environment, though modest dress is recommended to blend in culturally.
tips: Dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees, especially outside tourist areas
Use Yandex Go app for safe, tracked transportation instead of street taxis
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas for better security and amenities

nightSafety.overview: Tashkent maintains good safety levels after dark, particularly in central areas and well-lit districts. The city has active nightlife with bars and restaurants operating until late hours. Police presence is strong, and violent crime against tourists is very rare, though standard urban precautions are recommended.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas when walking at night
Travel in groups when possible and use ride-sharing apps for transportation
Be aware that some nightclub regulations require closure by 11 PM due to recent safety laws

transport.metro: Tashkent's Soviet-era metro system is safe, efficient, and beautiful with ornate station designs. Operates from early morning to late evening with frequent trains. Payment is cash-based and very affordable at approximately $0.12 per ride regardless of distance.
transport.rideshare: Yandex Go is the primary rideshare service with 86.3% market share. Safe, reliable, and tracks all rides. Payment possible by cash or card. International apps like Uber and Lyft don't operate in Uzbekistan.
transport.taxis: Official licensed taxis are generally safe, but always negotiate fare before departure. Unlicensed taxis pose risks of overcharging and rare but documented robbery incidents. Look for properly marked vehicles and official identification.
transport.tips: Download Yandex Go app upon arrival for safest transportation. Metro system is excellent for sightseeing and very secure. Avoid unmarked taxis and always verify driver identity matches app before entering vehicle.

customs: Greet with 'Assalomu alaykum' and respond with 'Alaykum assalom'; men shake hands while women typically nod
Remove shoes when entering homes and mosques; dress modestly covering shoulders and knees at religious sites
Use right hand for greetings, eating, and passing items; left hand is considered impolite for social interactions

health.overview: Healthcare quality in Tashkent is mixed, with private clinics offering better services than public facilities. The city has the country's best medical infrastructure, though many expats travel abroad for complex procedures. Basic medications are widely available without prescriptions.
health.water: Tap water is not recommended for drinking. Use bottled water, which is readily available and affordable throughout the city.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations recommended. No special vaccinations required for entry from most countries, but consult your doctor about hepatitis A/B and typhoid for extended stays.
health.altitude: Sea level elevation presents no altitude concerns. Climate ranges from very hot summers (35-40°C) to cold winters with snow. Spring and fall offer the most comfortable conditions.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Tashkent: 3 Moyqorghon Street, 5th block, Yunusobod District. Emergency: +998 781 205 450. Regular: +998 71 120 5450

faq.a: Yes, Tashkent is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and a US State Department Level 1 advisory. The city has strong security presence and locals are generally welcoming to visitors.

Tashkent is generally safe at night, especially in central areas and well-lit districts. Police presence is strong and violent crime is rare, though standard precautions like staying in groups and using main streets are recommended.

faq.q: Is Tashkent safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Tashkent is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, with many reporting feeling safer than in European capitals. Harassment is uncommon and locals are respectful, though modest dress is recommended for cultural sensitivity.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Tashkent?
faq.a: Industrial districts on the outskirts and some parts of Sergeli district have fewer security services. While not dangerous, these areas are less patrolled and poorly lit at night. The city center and main tourist areas are very safe.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Tashkent?
faq.a: Yes, public transport is very safe. The metro system is secure, efficient, and beautiful. For taxis, use the Yandex Go app for tracked, reliable service rather than unmarked street taxis which can pose overcharging risks.
